target: fj-api default wrapper backend allocation overhead
one_lever: Store default API wrapper backend names as borrowed Cow<'static, str> values while preserving owned custom backend overrides.

benchmark.command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-api --bench api_overhead -- 'api_composition|api_vs_dispatch|api_overhead' --sample-size 20 --measurement-time 3 --warm-up-time 1

score.rationale: Default API wrappers avoid one small heap allocation per construction on benchmark-visible strict CPU paths; multiple jit, grad, vmap, and composition midpoints improved.

behavior_proof.ordering: Transform order, ledger evidence IDs, dispatch call order, and backend resolution order are unchanged.
behavior_proof.tie_breaking: No comparator, map iteration, or extraction tie-breaker changed.
behavior_proof.floating_point: No primitive evaluation, AD arithmetic, or tolerance path changed.
behavior_proof.rng: No RNG primitive, seed, or state handling changed.
behavior_proof.strict_hardened: Compatibility mode and unknown-feature handling are unchanged.
behavior_proof.custom_backend: with_backend still stores an owned custom backend string and dispatch receives the same string content.

validation.notes: Exit 0; warning inventory remains in existing tests and known heuristic surfaces, with no critical findings.
88 unit tests, 45 api_transform_suite tests, bench harness smoke tests, and readme example test target passed.

validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-api --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings

rejected_alternatives: Static ledger evidence IDs: rejected because it touches audit/hash semantics.
Fixed-size transform stack storage: rejected as too much churn for a one-lever pass.
Benchmark-only hoisting: rejected because it does not optimize the API path.
